methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

data.narrative:
Near Space Labs, a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Rema Matevosyan, is evaluated against a peer set of 4 private companies in the same sector and stage (Aalyria, Stoke Space, Hubble Network, Sift) plus 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods triangulate to a range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the company's $40M+ total raised, implying $76M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 private comparables yields $700M, with individual valuations spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue figures, which is typical for private companies in this sector. The public Space & Aerospace universe trades at a 45.3x median P/S across 6 tickers, but lacks actionable leverage without target revenue.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $65M to $805M, with a median implied valuation of $388M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity in peer stage maturity and capital efficiency within frontier aerospace. Key uncertainties include whether Near Space Labs' technology scope, customer traction, or burn profile aligns with the higher-valued peers (Aalyria at $1.3B, Stoke at $1.0B) or lower-end comparables (Sift at $274M). Revenue disclosure would enable public-market cross-validation and narrow the band materially.
